content: nferris/tracewell: open-source evaluation harness for LLM agent pipelines — records agent traces, developers pin expected behaviors, CI fails when behavior drifts. Repo created 2026-05-14 (9 weeks old). stars=1420, 40+ external issue filers. Works today with LangGraph and CrewAI — verified against the repo's examples/ directory. README contains a benchmark table claiming drift-detection accuracy — not independently replicated.

velocity: commits_6w=42 active_days=42 window_since=2026-06-06 repo=nferris/tracewell | 61 consecutive days with commits (2026-05-16 to 2026-07-15). 3 tagged releases: v0.1.0 2026-05-28, v0.2.0 2026-06-18, v0.3.0 2026-07-09. Median issue response under 8 hours (computed over 40+ issues). 11 of the last 20 merged PRs authored by external contributors.

Show HN: Tracewell – CI-style regression tests for LLM agent behavior | author=nferris points=890 — #2 Show HN of its day. Submitter domain tracewell.dev matches the domain linked on the GitHub profile: identity match across the two sources.
PyPI package tracewell: ~3,100 weekly downloads. Registry stats — single source, no external corroboration.

HN comment thread on the Tracewell Show HN: commenters note the agent-eval category is early with no dominant incumbent; several name LangSmith and Braintrust as tools that could absorb this feature. Single-source market read.

GitHub profile nferris links personal domain tracewell.dev. No employment or education claims made anywhere public; searched public company registries for a Tracewell incorporation: no filing found — negative result, company not incorporated. Solo maintainer; no commercial track record found.
